"Welcome to [Microsoft Edge]. Please read the <link1>Microsoft Privacy Statement</link1> and the <link2>Third-party notices</link2> carefully to learn how we ensure the safety of your personal information during the process of collection, usage, storage and sharing, and the relevant rights you own. We take industry-leading security technical measures to fully protect your personal information.
You need to provide your email address or phone number to create an account and password, or login.
To browse and save local files, photos, and media, we will request for access to your device storage when you use such features. To search images or search voice, we will request for access to your device camera and microphone when you use such features. To provide the function of sharing location, we will request for access to your device location when you use such features. To provide you with the information you care about, we will collect your information of browsing history. You can access, rectify, and delete your personal information and manage your authorizations through [Settings].
We’ll collect your OAID and Android ID to do diagnostics when you encounter any technical difficulties and to do statistics of the overall usage of Microsoft Edge.
We'll collect your installed application list to provide sharing experience when you share website.
We’ll ask for your authorization before collecting them, and you can choose exit.
To ensure that the app can function properly according to the latest state of the system, even when it ifCan't s closed or running in the background, the app will still receive background broadcast information sent to each app and send you notifications if necessary (i.e., \"auto-launch\" or \"associated launch\") based on the broadcast information.
To support the normal function implementation of this product, multiple cookies and similar automatic collection tools will be used in the product. For details about the third-party services used by the product, such as SDK, you can read the <link3>Third-party notices</link3>.
Please click “I agree” to consent to our <bold>Privacy Statement</bold> and start to use the service."

"Enabling “Do Not Track” means that a request will be included with your browsing traffic. Any effect depends on whether a website responds to the request, and how the request is interpreted.
For example, some websites may respond to this request by showing you ads that aren’t based on other websites you’ve visited. Many websites will still collect and use your browsing data — for example to improve security, to provide content, ads and recommendations, and to generate reporting statistics."

What can you do with Copilot?
Discover more with these helpful links:

The Microsoft 365 Chat feature in Copilot combines the power of large language models (LLMs) with the intelligence of the Microsoft Graph to help you get things done. If authorized by your organization, you have the option to add plugins for more data sources (like apps and web content) that Copilot will interact with on your behalf.
Microsoft Copilot is your everyday AI companion with enterprise-grade security, privacy, and compliance.

Copilot can synthesize data from multiple sources to give you a summary of things you need to catch up on, including your files, messages, meetings, emails, and people. It can also help you find and use info that’s buried in documents or lost in conversations. And with Copilot by your side, you can create content with it all.

Microsoft Copilot combines the power of large language models (LLMs) with up-to-date information from the web to give you better answers, greater efficiency, and new ways to be creative. For example, get quick summaries of the content you view online, compare products to make informed business decisions, analyze images, draft content, and more.

Microsoft Edge will only collect data that you have consented to providing. While browsing InPrivate, the websites you have visited are never used for product improvement and are not associated with your Microsoft account.<link> Privacy statement</link>
What data does Microsoft Edge collect?

Microsoft Edge will delete your browsing history, cookies, and site data, as well as passwords, addresses, and form data when you close all InPrivate tabs. Other people using this device won’t see your browsing activity but your school, workplace, and internet service provider might still be able to access this data. Your favorites will be saved, so you can access them next time you use Microsoft Edge. <link>Learn more</link>
What can people see about my browsing?

When you browse InPrivate, your tracking prevention settings will remain the same as in a normal browsing tab. However, if you would like to always use “Strict” tracking prevention when you browse InPrivate, you can choose that in Settings. <link> Settings</link>

"Your organization, %1$s, manages the account you're signing in to and how Microsoft Edge can be used. Your administrator can set up or restrict certain features."

Your administrator can make changes to your profile and browser remotely, analyze information about the browser through reporting, and perform other necessary tasks. Activity on this device may also be managed outside of Microsoft Edge.
Your organization may be able to view:

When security events are flagged by Chrome, relevant data about the events is sent to your administrator. This can include URLs of pages you visit in Chrome, URLs of pages you copied data from, file names or metadata, IP addresses, browser or tab crashes, and the username that you use to sign in to web based applications, your device and Chrome.
The username on the device and the username on the browser
Version information about your device and browser
A limited list of URLs of pages you visit where legacy technology events are occuring
A limited list of URLs of pages you visit where legacy technology events are occuring

URLs of pages you visit are sent to Google Cloud or third parties for analysis, and may be visible to your administrator. For example, they might be scanned to detect unsafe websites or filter websites based on rules set by the administrator.

The profile you’re signed in to is a managed profile. Your administrator can make changes to your profile settings remotely, analyze information about the browser through reporting, and perform other necessary tasks.
Browser and device OS information (such as the browser & OS versions)

Applied browser policies in your work profile
Work profile information (such as your username)

Because the preloaded pages are encrypted, Microsoft will not learn anything about the preloaded page content. Microsoft servers will learn which sites are privately preloaded. This information is only used to preload the pages, and is not linked to other information from your Microsoft Account.
More frequently preloads pages that Edge thinks you are likely to visit. This setting may result in increased data use.
When a site or app asks to privately preload links on their page, Edge encrypts and preloads pages through Microsoft servers without cookies. This hides your identity from the preloaded site.
If you allow cookies, Edge may use them for preloading.
More pages are preloaded. Pages may be preloaded through Microsoft servers when requested by other sites or apps.
Pages load only after you open them
Because the preloaded pages are encrypted, and the site or app linking to the pages is a Microsoft site, Microsoft servers don’t receive new information when privately preloading these pages.
Preloads pages that Microsoft Edge thinks you are likely to visit.

When a Microsoft site or app asks to privately preload links on their page, Microsoft Edge encrypts and preloads pages through Microsoft servers without cookies. This hides your identity from the preloaded site.
If you allow cookies, Microsoft Edge may use them for preloading.
Some of the pages you visit are preloaded. Pages may be preloaded through Microsoft servers when linked from a Microsoft site or app.

Preloads pages that Microsoft Edge thinks you might visit. To do this, Microsoft Edge may use cookies, if you allow cookies, and may encrypt and send pages through Microsoft to hide your identity from sites.

Privacy Sandbox trials are still in active development and are available in selected regions. For now, sites may try out Privacy Sandbox while continuing to use current web technologies like third-party cookies."

Ad topics help sites show you relevant ads while protecting your browsing history and identity. Edge can note topics of interest based on your recent browsing history. Later, a site you visit can ask Edge for relevant topics to personalize the ads you see.

Ad topics help websites show you relevant ads while protecting your browsing history and identity. Microsoft Edge can note topics of interest based on your recent browsing history. Later, a site you visit can ask Microsoft Edge for relevant topics to personalize the ads you see.
You can see ad topics in settings and block the ones you don’t want shared with sites. Edge also auto-deletes ad topics that are older than 4 weeks.
You can change your mind any time in Edge settings
What data is used: Your ad topics are based on your recent browsing history, a list of sites you’ve visited using Edge on this device.

How we use this data: Edge notes topics of interest as you browse. Topic labels are predefined and include things like, Arts & Entertainment, Shopping, and Sports. Later, a site you visit can ask Edge for a few of your topics (but not your browsing history) to personalize the ads you see.

"Microsoft requires companies to state publicly that they won't use this data to track you across sites. Some sites may use your activity to personalize your experience for more than just ads. They may also combine it with other information they already know about you. Companies are responsible for letting you know how they use your data. <link>Learn more about how Microsoft protects your data in our Privacy Policy</link>"

How you can manage your data: Edge auto-deletes topics that are older than 4 weeks. As you keep browsing, a topic might reappear on the list. You can also block topics you don’t want Edge to share with sites and turn ad topics off at any time in Edge settings.
Learn more about how Microsoft protects your data in our Privacy Policy.
You can make changes in Edge settings.
What data is used: Your activity on a site you visit using Edge on this device.

How sites use this data: Sites can store information with Edge about things you like. For example, if you visit a site about marathon training, the site might decide that you’re interested in running shoes. Later, if you visit a different site, that site can show you an ad for running shoes suggested by the first site.

How you can manage your data: Edge auto-deletes sites that are older than 30 days. A site you visit again might reappear on the list. You can also block a site from suggesting ads for you and turn site-suggested ads off at any time in Edge settings.
Sites you visit can ask Edge for information that helps them measure the performance of their ads. Edge protects your privacy by limiting the information sites can share with one another.

"Microsoft requires companies to state publicly that they won't use this data to track you across sites. Some sites may use your activity to personalize your experience for more than just ads. They may also combine it with other information they already know about you. Companies are responsible for letting you know how they use your data. <link>Learn more about how Microsoft protects your data in our Privacy Policy</link>"

Edge notes topics of interest based on your recent browsing history. Also, sites you visit can determine what you like. Later, sites can ask for this information to show you personalized ads. You can choose which topics and sites are used to show you ads.
You can make changes in Edge settings.
Edge notes topics of interest based on your recent browsing history.

Edge auto-deletes topics and sites that suggest ads within 30 days. Or you can block specific topics and sites you don’t like.
Sites you visit can ask Edge for information to help them measure the performance of their ads. Edge lets sites collect limited types of data, such as the time of day an ad was shown to you.
Learn more about how Microsoft protects your data in our Privacy Policy.

"Microsoft requires companies to state publicly that they won't use this data to track you across sites. Some sites may use your activity to personalize your experience for more than just ads.
